channel.title: Self dot send (Computing Blog)
channel.description: Message passing is just a procedure call.

item.title: Lolcat in Arm Assembly
item.description: Rewriting x86 Lolcat in Arm assembly.

item.title: Writing a Programmers Editor (Undo/Redo & Command Log) - Part 10
item.description: Building undo and redo — from linear history to undo trees, and the command log pattern.

item.title: Writing a Programmers Editor (Modes & Extensibility) - Part 11
item.description: The extensibility layer — major modes, minor modes, hooks, and giving the user 100% power.

item.title: Writing a Programmers Editor (Terminal I/O & Raw Mode) - Part 5
item.description: How to take control of the terminal — raw mode, escape sequences, and turning a teletype into an interactive display.

item.title: Writing a Programmers Editor (Keymaps & Input Handling) - Part 6
item.description: Designing a keymap system — translating raw keystrokes into editor commands, and the foundation for modal editing.

item.title: Writing a Programmers Editor (Rendering & Redisplay) - Part 7
item.description: Efficient screen redisplay — dirty line tracking, differential rendering, and avoiding the full repaint.

item.title: Writing a Programmers Editor (Syntax Highlighting) - Part 9
item.description: Adding colour to code — tokenization, fontification, and how to build a syntax highlighting engine.

item.title: Writing a Programmers Editor - Part 1
item.description: A series of Assays on writing a programmers editor.

item.title: Writing a Programmers Editor (Gap Buffer in Scheme) - Part 3
item.description: Implementing a gap buffer from scratch in Scheme — the core data structure for our editor.

item.title: Writing a Programmers Editor (Lines & Display) - Part 4
item.description: Building a line management and display layer on top of the gap buffer.
